findings.title: Relative CODEX_HOME breaks global backup restore/uninstall target resolution
evidence_refs: manual_repro_2026-04-17: HOME=<tmp>/home CODEX_HOME=codex-home codex1 setup --json then restore/uninstall --backup-id <id> resolves paths under <repo>/codex-home/codex-home/* and reports already_absent while real <repo>/codex-home/* remains mutated
findings.rationale: Global setup accepts relative CODEX_HOME and, when that directory does not already exist, writes manifest repo_root/path entries as relative (for example `codex-home/config.toml`). Restore and uninstall then resolve user-scope entries by joining `user_root` with `entry.path`, producing duplicated paths (`.../codex-home/codex-home/...`). This causes restore/uninstall to operate on non-existent targets and return success/no-op actions (`already_absent`) while leaving actual managed files in place, violating backup/restore integrity and uninstall correctness.
findings.suggested_next_action: Normalize CODEX_HOME to an absolute canonical-or-normalized path before planning/writing manifests in global setup, and/or store user-scope manifest paths relative to user_root (not including the user_root prefix). Add targeted tests that run global setup+restore and setup+uninstall with a relative CODEX_HOME and assert real managed files are restored/removed and no duplicated codex-home path is produced.
